data.title: AMD Stock Gets New Street-High Price Target Among Several Hikes – Here's Why
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices' (NASDAQ:AMD) 2026 Advancing AI event appears to have made a big impression on Wall Street, with a host of Wall Street analysts raising their price targets following the latest announcements.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

At the event, AMD said its rack-scale AI platform, Helios, has entered full production. Shipments are expected to begin in September, with the rollout accelerating through the first half of 2027. The company identified Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta, Microsoft and Oracle as its initial customers.

AMD also substantially increased its outlook for the server CPU market, projecting growth from approximately $26 billion in 2025 to $220 billion by 2030. The company said the expansion will be driven by agentic AI workloads, which require significantly greater infrastructure to support orchestration, tool calling, data access and inference management. The new estimate is roughly $100 billion higher than AMD's previous projection of a server CPU TAM exceeding $120 billion by 2030, issued during its Q1 earnings call. It also slightly surpasses Nvidia's latest $200 billion estimate for the market.

Among analysts taking it all in, Baird's Tristan Gerra, ranked among the top 1% of Street stock pros, appeared to be the most bullish of the lot.

Gerra reiterated an Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating and raised his price target from $625 to a Street-high of $1,250, suggesting the stock will gain 132% over the coming year. "Raising our estimates to reflect strong growth in both AMD's AI GPU and AI CPU platforms. We are now modeling $147B in AI GPU revenue by 2030, assuming 15% market share," the 5-star analyst said. (To watch Gerra's track record, click here)

Meanwhile, UBS's Timothy Arcuri, ranked 2nd among the thousands of Street analysts, maintained a Buy rating on the shares and raised his price target from $700 to $730, implying 12-month upside of 35% from current levels. (To watch Arcuri's track record, click here)

Arcuri's "new view" is based on the possibility that AMD's 2028 EPS could approach $30, following the company's latest commentary on its CPU roadmap, market share ambitions and TAM at its AI Day. "We believe server CPU is going to grow so fast and, with gross margin that we think is ~1,000bps above corporate average, this could establish a margin "umbrella" under which AMD can price an impressively spec'd MI500 on the aggressive side in 2027 to take incrementally more share of the GPU TAM," the 5-star analyst went on to say.

Arcuri also said TSMC's recent increase in capital spending had improved his confidence in AMD's supply outlook, while server CPU revenue could reach approximately $35 billion in 2027 and potentially $60 billion in 2028. "AMD remains a favorite idea even after a ~75% move in the past 3 months," he summed up.

Melius analyst Ben Reitzes is just as effusive. Although AMD is scheduled to report earnings in less than two weeks, Reitzes said the company had already "clearly signaled" that its 2027 and long-term revenue potential is likely higher than consensus expectations. With OpenAI and Meta on track, he also sees greater potential for Anthropic-related revenue in 2027 than previously anticipated. "CPUs are simply on fire as well and the amount of revenue AMD plans to capture from agents is higher than expected," Reitzes said.

While his estimates are likely to rise following AMD's August 4 earnings report, the revenue growth CAGR of more than 35% through 2030 outlined at the November Analyst Day now appears "too conservative."

Accordingly, Reitzes reiterated a Buy rating on the shares and raised his price target from $540 to $660, implying the stock will deliver returns of 22% a year from now. (To watch Reitzes' track record, click here)

Elsewhere on the Street, the stock claims an additional 25 Buys and 8 Holds, for a Strong Buy consensus rating. At $569.44, the average price target suggests shares will add 5.5% in the months ahead. (See AMD stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Why Are Micron and SanDisk Stocks Falling in Pre-Market Today Despite Intel's Strong Earnings?
data.description:
Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) stocks moved lower in Friday's pre-market trading, even as Intel (INTC) surged after strong second-quarter results. The weakness in MU and SNDK comes as memory stocks remain under pressure after their recent pullback.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

SNDK has a long & short ETF? Explore SNXX & SNDQ

The moves also follow a sharp sell-off in South Korean chip stocks. The KOSPI fell over 6%, while Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) and SK Hynix (SKHY) dropped more than 7%. The steep losses also triggered trading halts in Seoul. This weakness appears to have spilled into U.S. memory stocks, putting pressure on Micron and SanDisk despite Intel's strong results.

Meanwhile, Intel stock jumped about 4% before the opening bell. Its strong results also lifted Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Arm Holdings (ARM), showing a clear split in Friday's early chip trading. At the same time, Micron fell nearly 2%, while SanDisk dropped over 3%.

Intel's Results Point to Strong AI Demand

Intel reported second-quarter revenue of $16.1 billion, up nearly 25% from last year. Adjusted EPS came in at $0.42, well above Wall Street's estimate of $0.22.

Its Data Center and AI sales jumped 59% to $6.3 billion. Intel also gave a better-than-expected outlook for the third quarter. The company expects revenue of $15.8 billion to $16.8 billion and EPS of $0.38.

The results point to solid demand for data-center chips. However, that strength has not been enough to lift all chip stocks today.

Why Are Memory Stocks Still Under Pressure?

Micron and SanDisk have seen sharp swings after a huge run in memory stocks. Investors have been weighing strong AI demand against concerns about high valuations and the risk of more chip supply over time. Still, Micron stock is up more than 240% in 2026, while SNDK has surged over 570% despite its recent pullback.

Still, Wall Street remains positive on the memory chip market. Five-star KeyBanc analyst John Vinh  expects memory supply to remain tight through 2027, which could support higher chip prices. This outlook could benefit memory chipmakers such as Micron and SanDisk.

Which Stock Does Wall Street See More Upside In?

Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, we compared Micron and SanDisk to see which stock offers more upside based on analysts' price targets.

Both stocks carry Strong Buy consensus ratings. Analysts see more upside in Micron. The average MU price target of $1,569.29 implies 58.48% upside, while SanDisk's average price target of $2,052.50 implies 27.46% upside.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Slips as AMD Challenges Dominance with Helios AI Server
data.description:
Shares in chipmaking giant Nvidia (NVDA) edged lower on Friday after rival Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) launched its long-awaited Helios rack-scale server system. This marks AMD's latest challenge to Nvidia's flagship products.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Explore NVDS for 2X short leverage on NVDA

AMD Steps Up Rivalry with Nvidia

AMD launched the new system on Thursday at its flagship Advancing AI 2026 event held in San Francisco, California. CEO Lisa Su also confirmed that the chipmaker has kicked off full production of the system, which was first introduced in 2025.

Microsoft (MSFT) and Anthropic were announced earlier this week as customers for the system. However, the U.S. chipmaker further revealed on Thursday that Meta (META), Oracle (ORCL), and OpenAI, among others, have also signed up to use the system. Meta has started using Helios to test its AI workload.

Nvidia Races to Deliver Vera Rubin

Helios combines AMD's latest Instinct MI455X GPUs and the EPYC "Venice" CPUs. It also runs on the chipmaker's Pensando Vulcano network interface cards and ROCm open software for running AI workloads. 

Helios is considered a rival to Nvidia's advanced Vera Rubin AI system. Nvidia introduced the system in January this year and, in March, launched the AI-tailored Vera CPU to deliver agentic AI capabilities to the servers.

Vera Rubin is also powered by the next-generation Rubin GPU and Nvidia's networking chips. The chipmaking giant recently kicked off full production of the platform. It is aiming for late-year deployment by customers.

Is AMD Ready to Take on Nvidia in GPU Market?

Earlier in the year, DA Davidson analyst Gil Luria questioned whether AMD could compete against Nvidia with its Instinct GPUs. Luria described AMD as a marginal player in the AI accelerator market that is "playing catch-up."

How this will play out remains to be seen. However, Jefferies analyst Blayne Curtis, in a new note released on Thursday, pointed to "a roadmap that potentially puts AMD ahead of Nvidia."

Curtis noted that AMD's Venice CPU extends its lead in the CPU market. The analyst reaffirmed his Buy rating on AMD and lifted his price target from $515 to $640. The new target implies about 19% upside.

Is Nvidia a Strong Buy Now?

On Wall Street, Nvidia's shares continue to boast a Strong Buy consensus rating from analysts. This is based on 36 Buys and one Hold issued over the past three months.

In addition, the average NVDA price target of $309.94 suggests about 48% upside in the months ahead (see NVDA stock forecast here).
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: UBS Says It's Time to Diversify — These Are Its 2 Stock Picks
data.description:
Wall Street pulled back on Thursday after the S&P 500 had spent recent weeks trading near record territory. The index fell 1.2%, while the Nasdaq dropped 2.15%, as investors digested disappointing earnings from several large tech companies, heavier AI spending plans, higher oil prices, and rising Treasury yields.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Despite Thursday's decline, the broader economic backdrop remains supportive. The U.S. economy continues to benefit from a healthy labor market, steady credit growth, and fiscal spending that has supported business investment and consumer demand. Those factors have also helped strengthen earnings outside the tech sector, leading many market strategists to believe the expansion still has room to continue.

That is the conclusion reached by the Chief Investment Office (CIO) at UBS. While the firm remains constructive on the long-term outlook for AI, it believes improving economic conditions should allow a broader group of companies and sectors to participate in the market's next move.

"The US economy remains resilient, and a solid labor market, strong credit creation, and fiscal support should all help boost earnings outside the AI complex and enable this year's equity rally to broaden further… While we remain confident in AI's growth story and continue to see attractive opportunities in semis and hardware, we have also highlighted that the next leg of equity gains is likely to be marked by a broadening of market leadership. Investors should ensure diversified exposure across sectors and regions," the CIO team noted.

UBS isn't just encouraging investors to diversify; it is also pointing them toward specific opportunities. The bank's analysts have highlighted two stocks they believe can benefit as market leadership broadens. Using the TipRanks platform, we looked up the names to see whether the rest of the Street shares that bullish view.

dLocal Limited (DLO)

The UBS CIO emphasized the importance of diversifying across both sectors and regions, and the firm's first pick fits the bill. Uruguay-based dLocal operates in the fast-growing payment processing industry and has grown into a $4 billion company in over a decade. Its success is built on the idea that global commerce still depends on local payment systems. Specializing in cross-border payments, dLocal enables businesses to seamlessly collect and send funds across emerging markets through its pay-in and payout platform. Its customers range from small businesses to global brands such as Amazon, Mailchimp, Shein, Shopify, and Dropbox.

Global payment processing is a huge business, and dLocal processed $41 billion worth of payments in 2025. The company handled over 1,000 payment methods, for more than 760 merchants in over 60 countries. dLocal's business is prominent in fast-growing regions, including Latin America, where it has a presence in 17 countries; in Asia, where it is used in the world's two most populous countries, India and China, along with Japan and smaller markets such as Thailand and Vietnam; and in Africa, which, as the company itself notes, will be home to one-fourth of the global population by 2050, and where the Sub-Saharan region already has the world's largest number of mobile money accounts.

This is dLocal's niche, then; the developing world, where buyers and sellers need flexible payment solutions, where middle classes are starting to integrate into the world's economies, and where future growth will be concentrated. By selecting dLocal as a stock for investors to buy, UBS is making good on something its chief investment office said: 'Investors should ensure diversified exposure across sectors and regions.' dLocal is the business personification of diversified exposure across regions.

One measure of dLocal's strength is its recent addition to the Russell 2000 index. The addition was effective on this past June 29, and marked an important recognition of the company for its platform, its scale, and its strong execution.

That execution was visible in the company's 1Q26 report. dLocal processed $14.1 billion in the first quarter – stated in US currency – and generated revenues of $335.86 million. That revenue total was up an impressive 55% year-over-year, and beat expectations by $4.67 million. At the bottom line, the company's non-GAAP EPS of $0.17 was in line with the estimates.

Those strong fundamentals and long-term growth opportunities have made UBS analyst Kaio Prato bullish on the stock.

"We see a combination of strong growth path, with improving operating leverage and re-rating potential given the rising market confidence after a series of solid results. Growth should be supported by rising global digitalization and still lower e-commerce penetration in EM, expansion into new geographies and deeper relationships with clients, implying higher share of wallet. At the same time, we believe profitability should expand in 2H26, as DLO reaps fruits from the investment cycle deployed during 2024-25. We incorporate those trends and increase our earnings estimates by +15%, on avg., over 2026-30E… Adjusting for growth, we believe the stock trades at a compelling multiple," Prato opined.

Prado goes on to put a Buy rating on DLO shares, along with a $20 price target that implies a one-year upside potential of 42%. (To watch Prato's track record, click here)

Wall Street is largely on board with the bullish outlook. DLO earns a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 5 recent analyst reviews, including 4 Buys and just one Hold. The stock is priced at $14.08 right now, and its $17.38 average target price indicates room for a gain of 23% by this time next year. (See DLO stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Shareholders Sue Microsoft Over Claims That Copilot AI is "Best in Class"
data.description:
A class action lawsuit was filed against tech titan Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on June 12, 2026.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Plaintiffs in the federal securities lawsuit allege that they acquired Microsoft stock at artificially inflated prices between May 1, 2025, and January 28, 2026, known as the Class Period. They are now seeking compensation for financial losses incurred upon public revelation of the company's alleged misconduct during that time. To learn whether you may be eligible for a recovery under this securities lawsuit, click here.

What Does Microsoft Do?

Microsoft is a multinational technology conglomerate headquartered in Redmond, Washington. The company's offerings span enterprise software, cloud computing (including its Azure platform), online services, gaming, and technological hardware.

In recent years, Azure has served as Microsoft's primary growth driver, and the company has invested heavily in artificial intelligence capabilities, including over $13 billion in OpenAI and a commitment to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic, while launching its proprietary generative AI assistant, Copilot, across consumer and enterprise applications.

Why are Shareholders Suing Microsoft?

A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Microsoft Corporation and four of its senior executives on behalf of investors who purchased Microsoft common stock between May 1, 2025 and January 28, 2026. The complaint alleges that throughout the Class Period, defendants made materially false and misleading statements about the success, adoption, and competitive positioning of Microsoft's Copilot family of AI products, while concealing significant brand positioning failures, user experience problems, declining market share, and the need to divert billions of dollars in computing capacity away from profitable Azure cloud services to address Copilot's shortcomings.

Throughout the Class Period, the plaintiffs said that these executives repeatedly told investors that Copilot enjoyed "best-in-class" capabilities, that customer adoption was accelerating at record pace, and that Microsoft was uniquely positioned to lead the AI transformation of enterprise and consumer software.

The complaint alleges these statements failed to disclose that Copilot had experienced significant brand positioning, user experience, usage, data siloing, computational capacity, organizational, and interoperability problems. Plaintiffs allege that Microsoft's flagship proprietary AI model ranked well below competitors on benchmark tests, that confusing product versions and forced integration were frustrating users, and that organizational silos between consumer-focused and enterprise-focused Copilot teams prevented a coherent product vision.

When the alleged truth began to emerge through Microsoft's disappointing fiscal second quarter 2026 earnings on January 28, 2026, and subsequent investigative reporting, MSFT shares fell over $48 per share in a single day and continued declining to a low of $380 per share by March 20, 2026, representing a 30% decline from the Class Period high.

Indeed, MSFT shares have fallen 10% in the last three months – see below:
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 7/24/26 – Futures Edge Higher Despite Elevated Oil Prices
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher ahead of Friday's session after a sharp selloff on Wall Street, as investors weighed elevated oil prices and escalating U.S.-Iran tensions. Brent crude briefly climbed above $100 a barrel on Thursday for the first time in two months before retreating, fueling concerns about potential oil supply disruptions.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.11%, 0.23%, and 0.50%, respectively, at 5:33 a.m. EDT on July 24.

Oil prices were set for weekly gains after Houthi attacks on tankers in the Red Sea raised concerns about disruptions to another important oil shipping route. Kazakhstan's temporary production cuts also supported prices. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was down 2.68% at $93.97 a barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) slipped 3.39% at $89.66 a barrel.

On Thursday, major U.S. stock indices fell sharply as investors worried about rising AI spending following earnings reports from Tesla (TSLA) and Alphabet (GOOGL). The Nasdaq dropped 2.15%, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.21% and 0.97%, respectively. The major indices are now on track for weekly losses, with the Dow, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq down 0.8%, 0.7%, and 1.5%, respectively.

The benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield stood at 4.68% as of the last check. Additionally, the spot gold price in U.S. dollars traded around $4,053.90 per ounce on Friday.

On the earnings front, Verizon (VZ), American Express (AXP), NextEra Energy (NEE), and SLB (SLB) are scheduled to report results today.

Elsewhere, European markets opened higher today as investors reacted to the region's corporate earnings reports.

Asia-Pacific Markets Close Lower

Asia-Pacific markets closed lower after U.S. President Donald Trump imposed a 12.5% tariff on imports from Australia, China, Hong Kong, Singapore, and South Korea. The U.S. said the countries had failed to prevent goods made with forced labor from entering the American market.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 0.98%. In China, the Shanghai Composite slumped 1.61%, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 2.85%. In Japan, the Nikkei declined 2.73%, while the Topix lost 1.05%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Ethereum's Price Rally Catalysts Build as Bitwise Notes that Record Staking Is Restricting Supply
data.description:
Ethereum's (ETH-USD) revenue from network fees dropped 51% year-on-year to $64 million in the second quarter. However, ETH's underlying network activity and staking numbers have reached record levels during the same period. This has created a stronger foundation for a future ETH price rally, according to a report from Bitwise.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The asset manager explained that lower fees came from expanded network capacity. This made transactions cheaper for users instead of showing that people lost interest. This type of growing adoption often acts as an engine for ETH price growth over longer periods.

Cheaper Network Fees Could Help Boost ETH's Price

During the second quarter of 2025, Ethereum generated about $131 million in fee revenue. One year later, dollar revenue stood near $64 million.

At the same time, the actual usage of the Ethereum network grew a lot. Ethereum handled 203.9 million total transactions during the quarter, up from 121.1 million a year ago. Network speed also jumped from 15 to 26 transactions per second.

Highlighting this trend, researchers at Bitwise noted, "The divergence between revenue and activity is the theme of the quarter. While fees fell, usage climbed." Lower transaction fees make the Ethereum network easier and cheaper to use, which helps bring in new users and supports long-term price growth for ETH.

Surging User Activity Could Fuel ETH's Long-Term Demand

The Ethereum network recently expanded its capacity, making room for far more transactions. While cheaper fees temporarily reduced the Ethereum network's total fee revenue, the lower costs encouraged far more people to use the blockchain every day.

When measured in Ether, quarterly network revenue actually increased from 27,670 ETH in the first quarter to 31,166 ETH in the second quarter. Dollar revenue totals fell mainly because the market price of ETH weakened during the period.

Other crypto networks saw similar trends. Solana processed nearly 10 billion user transactions while its fee collection fell to $51 million. Avalanche handled 236 million transactions, up from 58 million a year ago. High transaction activity is usually a sign that token prices can grow over time.

33% of the Total ETH Supply Is Being Staked

Staking totals also grew as investors locked up more coins to earn yield. Active staked Ethereum reached a record 40.2 million ETH, representing roughly 33% of the total supply.

Large institutions helped drive the surge. Large fund managers staked more ETH, and this removed a lot of Ether coins from public supply. When available supply of a token falls and network usage climbs, even light buying can send prices higher quickly.

At the time of writing, Ethereum's price is sitting at $1,888.20.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Strategy Stock Ticks Up on New Report as Saylor Says 'Bitcoin Capital Markets Require a New Financial Language'
data.description:
Strategy stock (MSTR) rose 1.7% in pre-market trading after the firm posted new data showing $22 billion of its $58 billion Bitcoin (BTC-USD) holdings belong to lenders. This leaves $36 billion in Bitcoin for regular Strategy shareholders. Investors welcomed the breakdown because it separates the $22 billion owed to lenders from the actual value of Strategy stock.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Explaining why his firm updated its online reports, Michael Saylor, the firm's founder and executive chairman, shared his view, saying, "Bitcoin Capital Markets require a new financial language." By this, he means standard accounting methods cannot clearly show how companies borrow money to buy crypto.

Strategy Reveals Its Real Debt Figure

Strategy owns about 843,775 Bitcoin, making it the largest corporate holder of the coin. On paper, its live dashboard values that total pile at around $58 billion.

However, Strategy owes about $22 billion to lenders. These lenders have first rights to Strategy's money. Subtracting that debt leaves roughly $36 billion worth of Bitcoin for regular Strategy stock owners, which Strategy now calls its net reserve.

Strategy's Debt Acts like a Multiplier on MSTR's Stock Price

Borrowing money to buy crypto adds extra risk for normal Strategy stock owners. Paying back those loans and special investors costs Strategy about $1.8 billion every single year.

This debt acts like a multiplier on the stock price. When Bitcoin goes up, Strategy stock rises faster. When Bitcoin falls, Strategy stock drops much harder. Over the past year, Strategy stock dropped 77%, while Bitcoin fell 45%.

Strategy Is Improving Transparency for its Investors

In the past, Strategy's main reports did not show how much money went to lenders first. This made it look like regular shareholders owned the entire Bitcoin pile.

The new data clears up this confusion so that investors can see the true value backing Strategy shares. Strategy still plans to keep buying Bitcoin while owing its lenders first. The main difference now is that everyday investors can clearly see how much of that Bitcoin actually belongs to them. In short, Strategy is being more transparent with investors.

Is Strategy Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

According to TipRanks, Strategy (formerly known as MicroStrategy) stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 14 Wall Street analysts. This rating is based on 13 Buys and one Hold rating assigned in the past three months. The average 12-month MSTR price target of $275.46 implies 181% upside from current levels. (See MSTR stock forecast)
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Elon Musk Slams OpenAI's 'Non-Profit' Status for Being The 'Exact Opposite' of What He Funded
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) & SpaceX (SPCX) CEO Elon Musk said that OpenAI changed into an $800 billion closed-source business. He called OpenAI's shift the "exact opposite" of what he funded.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Two ETFs for long or short leverage on SPCX

Speaking with The Economist, Musk shared his issue with OpenAI, saying, "If you started a non-profit that was meant to be an open source AI company owned by the world and it somehow got turned into an $800 billion for-profit company with closed source, I think you'd be like, 'well, wait a second, that's the exact opposite of what I donated the money for.' That's my issue. I think it's a legitimate one."

His comments show why he continues his legal fight against OpenAI.

Musk Wanted OpenAI to Be a Free Alternative

Musk helped launch OpenAI in 2015 to create a free alternative to Google's (GOOGL) technology. He gave less than $45 million to get OpenAI moving before he stepped away in 2018.

OpenAI changed its plan completely late last year when OpenAI changed into a for-profit business. Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) bought a 27% stake in OpenAI. Today, TipRanks data estimates put OpenAI's overall value at $750 billion, $50 million less than what Musk's estimate is.
data.author: Annika Masrani
